segments.text: Forasmuch then as Christ hath suffered for us in the flesh,
arm yourselves likewise with the same mind: for he that hath suffered in the flesh hath ceased from sin;
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: That he no longer should live the rest of his time in the flesh to the lusts of men, but to the will of God.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: For the time past of our life may suffice us to have wrought the will of the Gentiles, when we walked in lasciviousness, lusts, excess of wine, revellings, banquetings, and abominable idolatries:
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Wherein they think it strange that ye run not with them to the same excess of riot,
speaking evil of you:
segments.speaker: Unbelievers

segments.text: Who shall give account to him that is ready to judge the quick and the dead.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: For for this cause was the gospel preached also to them that are dead, that they might be judged according to men in the flesh, but live according to God in the spirit.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: But the end of all things is at hand: be ye therefore sober, and watch unto prayer.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: And above all things have fervent charity among yourselves: for charity shall cover the multitude of sins.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Use hospitality one to another without grudging.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: As every man hath received the gift, even so minister the same one to another, as good stewards of the manifold grace of God.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: If any man speak, let him speak as the oracles of God; if any man minister, let him do it as of the ability which God giveth: that God in all things may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom be praise and dominion for ever and ever. Amen.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Beloved, think it not strange concerning the fiery trial which is to try you, as though some strange thing happened unto you:
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: But rejoice, inasmuch as ye are partakers of Christ's sufferings; that, when his glory shall be revealed, ye may be glad also with exceeding joy.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: If ye be reproached for the name of Christ, happy are ye; for the spirit of glory and of God resteth upon you: on their part he is evil spoken of, but on your part he is glorified.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: But let none of you suffer as a murderer, or as a thief, or as an evildoer, or as a busybody in other men's matters.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Yet if any man suffer as a Christian, let him not be ashamed; but let him glorify God on this behalf.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: For the time is come that judgment must begin at the house of God: and if it first begin at us, what shall the end be of them that obey not the gospel of God?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: And if the righteous scarcely be saved, where shall the ungodly and the sinner appear?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Wherefore let them that suffer according to the will of God commit the keeping of their souls to him in well doing, as unto a faithful Creator.
segments.speaker: Peter
